parts.text: /review Verify latest fixes for provider success=false handling and env error classification in workspace-deploy-provider.ts. Report blocking issues only.

state.output:
<path>/Users/nickdejesus/Code/nimbus/packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import type { Env, WorkspaceDeploymentStatus } from '../types.js';
2: 
3: export type WorkspaceDeployProviderName = 'simulated' | 'cloudflare_workers_assets';
4: 
5: export interface WorkspaceDeployProviderPrecheck {
6:   code: string;
7:   ok: boolean;
8:   details?: string;
9: }
10: 
11: export interface WorkspaceDeployCreateInput {
12:   workspaceId: string;
13:   deploymentId: string;
14:   outputDir: string;
15:   outputBundle: {
16:     bytes: Uint8Array;
17:     sha256: string;
18:   };
19: }
20: 
21: export interface WorkspaceDeployCreateResult {
22:   providerDeploymentId: string;
23:   status: WorkspaceDeploymentStatus;
24:   deployedUrl: string | null;
25: }
26: 
27: export interface WorkspaceDeployStatusResult {
28:   status: WorkspaceDeploymentStatus;
29:   deployedUrl: string | null;
30:   errorCode?: string;
31:   errorMessage?: string;
32: }
33: 
34: export interface WorkspaceDeployProvider {
35:   readonly name: WorkspaceDeployProviderName;
36:   precheck(): Promise<WorkspaceDeployProviderPrecheck[]>;
37:   createDeployment(input: WorkspaceDeployCreateInput): Promise<WorkspaceDeployCreateResult>;
38:   getDeploymentStatus(providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<WorkspaceDeployStatusResult>;
39:   cancelDeployment(providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<{ accepted: boolean }>;
40: }
41: 
42: class ProviderError extends Error {
43:   constructor(
44:     public readonly code:
45:       | 'provider_auth_failed'
46:       | 'provider_rate_limited'
47:       | 'provider_project_not_found'
48:       | 'provider_deploy_failed'
49:       | 'provider_invalid_output_dir'
50:       | 'provider_scope_missing',
51:     message: string
52:   ) {
53:     super(message);
54:     this.name = 'ProviderError';
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: type ProviderFetch = typeof fetch;
59: 
60: let providerFetch: ProviderFetch = fetch;
61: 
62: export function setWorkspaceDeployProviderFetchForTests(fetchImpl: ProviderFetch | null): void {
63:   providerFetch = fetchImpl ?? fetch;
64: }
65: 
66: function parseBoolean(value: string | undefined, fallback: boolean): boolean {
67:   if (value === undefined) {
68:     return fallback;
69:   }
70:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
71:   if (normalized === 'true' || normalized === '1' || normalized === 'yes' || normalized === 'on') {
72:     return true;
73:   }
74:   if (normalized === 'false' || normalized === '0' || normalized === 'no' || normalized === 'off') {
75:     return false;
76:   }
77:   return fallback;
78: }
79: 
80: function requireEnv(
81:   value: string | undefined,
82:   key: string,
83:   code: 'provider_auth_failed' | 'provider_scope_missing' = 'provider_auth_failed'
84: ): string {
85:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim() === '') {
86:     throw new ProviderError(code, `Missing required provider env: ${key}`);
87:   }
88:   return value.trim();
89: }
90: 
91: function cloudflareErrorsMessage(input: unknown): string | null {
92:   if (!Array.isArray(input)) {
93:     return null;
94:   }
95:   const messages = input
96:     .map((item) => {
97:       if (!item || typeof item !== 'object') {
98:         return null;
99:       }
100:       const record = item as Record<string, unknown>;
101:       if (typeof record.message === 'string' && record.message.trim()) {
102:         return record.message.trim();
103:       }
104:       return null;
105:     })
106:     .filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value));
107:   if (messages.length === 0) {
108:     return null;
109:   }
110:   return messages.join('; ');
111: }
112: 
113: function isValidRelativeOutputDir(value: string): boolean {
114:   if (!value || value === '.') {
115:     return false;
116:   }
117:   if (value.startsWith('/') || value.includes('..') || value.includes('\\')) {
118:     return false;
119:   }
120:   return value.split('/').every((segment) => Boolean(segment) && segment !== '.');
121: }
122: 
123: function mapProviderState(input: string): WorkspaceDeploymentStatus {
124:   const normalized = input.trim().toLowerCase();
125:   if (normalized === 'queued' || normalized === 'pending') {
126:     return 'queued';
127:   }
128:   if (normalized === 'running' || normalized === 'in_progress' || normalized === 'processing') {
129:     return 'running';
130:   }
131:   if (normalized === 'succeeded' || normalized === 'success' || normalized === 'completed' || normalized === 'active') {
132:     return 'succeeded';
133:   }
134:   if (normalized === 'cancelled' || normalized === 'canceled') {
135:     return 'cancelled';
136:   }
137:   return 'failed';
138: }
139: 
140: class SimulatedWorkspaceDeployProvider implements WorkspaceDeployProvider {
141:   readonly name = 'simulated' as const;
142: 
143:   constructor(private readonly env: Env) {}
144: 
145:   async precheck(): Promise<WorkspaceDeployProviderPrecheck[]> {
146:     return [{ code: 'provider_simulated', ok: true, details: 'simulated provider selected' }];
147:   }
148: 
149:   async createDeployment(input: WorkspaceDeployCreateInput): Promise<WorkspaceDeployCreateResult> {
150:     const deployBase = (this.env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_BASE_URL ?? 'https://deployments.nimbus.local').replace(/\/+$/, '');
151:     return {
152:       providerDeploymentId: input.deploymentId,
153:       status: 'succeeded',
154:       deployedUrl: `${deployBase}/${input.workspaceId}/${input.deploymentId}`,
155:     };
156:   }
157: 
158:   async getDeploymentStatus(_providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<WorkspaceDeployStatusResult> {
159:     return { status: 'succeeded', deployedUrl: null };
160:   }
161: 
162:   async cancelDeployment(_providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<{ accepted: boolean }> {
163:     return { accepted: true };
164:   }
165: }
166: 
167: class CloudflareWorkersAssetsProvider implements WorkspaceDeployProvider {
168:   readonly name = 'cloudflare_workers_assets' as const;
169:   private readonly accountId: string;
170:   private readonly apiToken: string;
171:   private readonly previewDomain: string;
172:   private readonly projectName: string;
173: 
174:   constructor(private readonly env: Env) {
175:     this.accountId = requireEnv(env.CF_ACCOUNT_ID, 'CF_ACCOUNT_ID');
176:     this.apiToken = requireEnv(env.CF_API_TOKEN, 'CF_API_TOKEN');
177:     this.previewDomain = requireEnv(
178:       env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN,
179:       'WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN',
180:       'provider_scope_missing'
181:     );
182:     this.projectName = requireEnv(
183:       env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME,
184:       'WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME',
185:       'provider_scope_missing'
186:     );
187: 
188:     if (!parseBoolean(env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED, false)) {
189:       throw new ProviderError(
190:         'provider_scope_missing',
191:         'Real deploy provider is disabled; set WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED=true to enable'
192:       );
193:     }
194:   }
195: 
196:   private request(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {
197:     return providerFetch(`https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4${path}`, {
198:       ...init,
199:       headers: {
200:         Authorization: `Bearer ${this.apiToken}`,
201:         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
202:         ...(init?.headers ?? {}),
203:       },
204:     });
205:   }
206: 
207:   private async parseCloudflareResult(response: Response): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
208:     const text = await response.text();
209:     let parsed: Record<string, unknown> = {};
210:     try {
211:       parsed = JSON.parse(text) as Record<string, unknown>;
212:     } catch {
213:       parsed = {};
214:     }
215: 
216:     if (response.status === 401) {
217:       throw new ProviderError('provider_auth_failed', 'Cloudflare API token is invalid');
218:     }
219:     if (response.status === 403) {
220:       throw new ProviderError('provider_scope_missing', 'Cloudflare API token is missing required scopes');
221:     }
222:     if (response.status === 404) {
223:       throw new ProviderError('provider_project_not_found', `Cloudflare project ${this.projectName} was not found`);
224:     }
225:     if (response.status === 429) {
226:       throw new ProviderError('provider_rate_limited', 'Cloudflare API rate limit exceeded');
227:     }
228:     if (!response.ok) {
229:       const errorMessage =
230:         cloudflareErrorsMessage(parsed.errors) ??
231:         (typeof parsed.errors === 'object' && parsed.errors !== null
232:           ? JSON.stringify(parsed.errors)
233:           : text || `Cloudflare API request failed with status ${response.status}`);
234:       throw new ProviderError('provider_deploy_failed', errorMessage);
235:     }
236: 
237:     if (parsed.success === false) {
238:       const errorMessage =
239:         cloudflareErrorsMessage(parsed.errors) ??
240:         `Cloudflare API reported success=false${response.status ? ` (status ${response.status})` : ''}`;
241:       throw new ProviderError('provider_deploy_failed', errorMessage);
242:     }
243: 
244:     return parsed;
245:   }
246: 
247:   private previewUrlForDeployment(deploymentId: string): string {
248:     return `https://dep-${deploymentId}.${this.previewDomain}`;
249:   }
250: 
251:   async precheck(): Promise<WorkspaceDeployProviderPrecheck[]> {
252:     const checks: WorkspaceDeployProviderPrecheck[] = [];
253:     checks.push({ code: 'provider_real_enabled', ok: true });
254:     checks.push({ code: 'provider_credentials_present', ok: true });
255: 
256:     const response = await this.request(`/accounts/${this.accountId}/workers/scripts/${this.projectName}`);
257:     await this.parseCloudflareResult(response);
258:     checks.push({ code: 'provider_project_access', ok: true });
259:     return checks;
260:   }
261: 
262:   async createDeployment(input: WorkspaceDeployCreateInput): Promise<WorkspaceDeployCreateResult> {
263:     if (!isValidRelativeOutputDir(input.outputDir)) {
264:       throw new ProviderError(
265:         'provider_invalid_output_dir',
266:         'deploy.outputDir must be a safe non-root relative directory path'
267:       );
268:     }
269: 
270:     const response = await this.request(`/accounts/${this.accountId}/workers/scripts/${this.projectName}/deployments`, {
271:       method: 'POST',
272:       body: JSON.stringify({
273:         deployment_id: input.deploymentId,
274:         alias: `dep-${input.deploymentId}`,
275:         output_dir: input.outputDir,
276:         artifact_sha256: input.outputBundle.sha256,
277:         artifact_bytes: input.outputBundle.bytes.byteLength,
278:         preview_url: this.previewUrlForDeployment(input.deploymentId),
279:       }),
280:     });
281:     const parsed = await this.parseCloudflareResult(response);
282:     const result =
283:       typeof parsed.result === 'object' && parsed.result !== null ? (parsed.result as Record<string, unknown>) : {};
284:     const providerDeploymentId =
285:       typeof result.id === 'string' && result.id.trim() ? result.id.trim() : `cfdep_${input.deploymentId}`;
286: 
287:     return {
288:       providerDeploymentId,
289:       status: 'running',
290:       deployedUrl: this.previewUrlForDeployment(input.deploymentId),
291:     };
292:   }
293: 
294:   async getDeploymentStatus(providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<WorkspaceDeployStatusResult> {
295:     const response = await this.request(
296:       `/accounts/${this.accountId}/workers/scripts/${this.projectName}/deployments/${providerDeploymentId}`
297:     );
298:     const parsed = await this.parseCloudflareResult(response);
299:     const result =
300:       typeof parsed.result === 'object' && parsed.result !== null ? (parsed.result as Record<string, unknown>) : {};
301:     const state = typeof result.status === 'string' ? mapProviderState(result.status) : 'running';
302:     return {
303:       status: state,
304:       deployedUrl:
305:         typeof result.preview_url === 'string' && result.preview_url.trim()
306:           ? result.preview_url
307:           : this.previewUrlForDeployment(providerDeploymentId.replace(/^cfdep_/, '')),
308:       errorCode: typeof result.error_code === 'string' ? result.error_code : undefined,
309:       errorMessage: typeof result.error_message === 'string' ? result.error_message : undefined,
310:     };
311:   }
312: 
313:   async cancelDeployment(providerDeploymentId: string): Promise<{ accepted: boolean }> {
314:     const response = await this.request(
315:       `/accounts/${this.accountId}/workers/scripts/${this.projectName}/deployments/${providerDeploymentId}/cancel`,
316:       { method: 'POST', body: '{}' }
317:     );
318: 
319:     if (response.status === 404 || response.status === 409) {
320:       return { accepted: false };
321:     }
322: 
323:     await this.parseCloudflareResult(response);
324:     return { accepted: true };
325:   }
326: }
327: 
328: export function getWorkspaceDeployProviderName(input: unknown, env: Env): WorkspaceDeployProviderName {
329:   if (typeof input === 'string' && input.trim()) {
330:     if (input === 'simulated' || input === 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
331:       return input;
332:     }
333:   }
334: 
335:   if (env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROVIDER === 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
336:     return 'cloudflare_workers_assets';
337:   }
338: 
339:   return 'simulated';
340: }
341: 
342: export function createWorkspaceDeployProvider(providerName: WorkspaceDeployProviderName, env: Env): WorkspaceDeployProvider {
343:   if (providerName === 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
344:     return new CloudflareWorkersAssetsProvider(env);
345:   }
346:   return new SimulatedWorkspaceDeployProvider(env);
347: }
348: 
349: export function normalizeProviderError(error: unknown): { code: string; message: string } {
350:   if (error instanceof ProviderError) {
351:     return { code: error.code, message: error.message };
352:   }
353: 
354:   if (error instanceof Error) {
355:     return { code: 'provider_deploy_failed', message: error.message };
356:   }
357: 
358:   return { code: 'provider_deploy_failed', message: String(error) };
359: }

(End of file - total 359 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { strict as assert } from 'assert';
2: import {
3:   createWorkspaceDeployProvider,
4:   getWorkspaceDeployProviderName,
5:   normalizeProviderError,
6:   setWorkspaceDeployProviderFetchForTests,
7: } from './workspace-deploy-provider.js';
8: 
9: export async function runWorkspaceDeployProviderTests(): Promise<void> {
10:   {
11:     const provider = createWorkspaceDeployProvider('simulated', {} as never);
12:     const checks = await provider.precheck();
13:     assert.equal(checks.length > 0, true);
14:     const created = await provider.createDeployment({
15:       workspaceId: 'ws_abc12345',
16:       deploymentId: 'dep_abcd1234',
17:       outputDir: 'dist',
18:       outputBundle: { bytes: new Uint8Array([1, 2, 3]), sha256: 'abc' },
19:     });
20:     assert.equal(created.status, 'succeeded');
21:   }
22: 
23:   {
24:     const resolved = getWorkspaceDeployProviderName(undefined, { WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROVIDER: 'cloudflare_workers_assets' } as never);
25:     assert.equal(resolved, 'cloudflare_workers_assets');
26:   }
27: 
28:   {
29:     setWorkspaceDeployProviderFetchForTests(async () =>
30:       new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: true, result: {} }), { status: 200 })
31:     );
32:     const provider = createWorkspaceDeployProvider('cloudflare_workers_assets', {
33:       CF_ACCOUNT_ID: 'acc',
34:       CF_API_TOKEN: 'token',
35:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN: 'preview.example.com',
36:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME: 'project',
37:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED: 'true',
38:     } as never);
39:     const checks = await provider.precheck();
40:     assert.equal(checks.every((check) => check.ok), true);
41:   }
42: 
43:   {
44:     setWorkspaceDeployProviderFetchForTests(async () =>
45:       new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: false, errors: [{ message: 'invalid request' }] }), { status: 200 })
46:     );
47:     const provider = createWorkspaceDeployProvider('cloudflare_workers_assets', {
48:       CF_ACCOUNT_ID: 'acc',
49:       CF_API_TOKEN: 'token',
50:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN: 'preview.example.com',
51:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME: 'project',
52:       WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED: 'true',
53:     } as never);
54:     await assert.rejects(provider.precheck(), /invalid request/);
55:   }
56: 
57:   {
58:     assert.throws(
59:       () =>
60:         createWorkspaceDeployProvider('cloudflare_workers_assets', {
61:           CF_ACCOUNT_ID: 'acc',
62:           CF_API_TOKEN: 'token',
63:           WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME: 'project',
64:           WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED: 'true',
65:         } as never),
66:       /WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN/
67:     );
68:   }
69: 
70:   {
71:     const normalized = normalizeProviderError(new Error('boom'));
72:     assert.equal(normalized.code, 'provider_deploy_failed');
73:   }
74: 
75:   setWorkspaceDeployProviderFetchForTests(null);
76: }

(End of file - total 76 lines)
</content>

115: function nextActionForDeploymentError(code: string | undefined): string | null {

123:     case 'validation_tool_missing':

126:       return 'Review test/build output, fix project errors, and retry deploy.';
127:     case 'invalid_project_root':
128:       return 'Set workspace source project root to a safe relative path and retry deploy.';

151: async function sha256Hex(input: string): Promise<string> {
152:   const digest = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', new TextEncoder().encode(input));
153:   return Array.from(new Uint8Array(digest))
154:     .map((byte) => byte.toString(16).padStart(2, '0'))
155:     .join('');
156: }

<content>1: import type { Env } from '../types.js';
2: import { loadRuntimeFlags } from '../lib/flags.js';

4:   createWorkspaceDeployProvider,

6:   normalizeProviderError,

8: 
9: const corsHeaders = {
10:   'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
11:   'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS',
12:   'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Idempotency-Key',
13: };
14: 
15: function jsonResponse(body: unknown, status = 200): Response {
16:   return new Response(JSON.stringify(body), {
17:     status,
18:     headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...corsHeaders },
19:   });
20: }
21: 
22: async function getTableColumns(env: Env, tableName: string): Promise<string[]> {
23:   const result = await env.DB.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all<Record<string, unknown>>();
24:   const rows = Array.isArray(result.results) ? result.results : [];
25:   return rows
26:     .map((row) => (typeof row.name === 'string' ? row.name : null))
27:     .filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value));
28: }
29: 
30: async function tableExists(env: Env, tableName: string): Promise<boolean> {
31:   const record = await env.DB.prepare('SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = ? AND name = ? LIMIT 1')
32:     .bind('table', tableName)
33:     .first<{ name: string }>();
34:   return Boolean(record?.name);
35: }
36: 
37: export async function handleGetDeployReadiness(env: Env): Promise<Response> {
38:   const checks: Array<{ code: string; ok: boolean; details?: string }> = [];
39: 
40:   checks.push({
41:     code: 'queue_binding_workspace_deploys',
42:     ok: Boolean(env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE),
43:     details: env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE ? 'binding detected' : 'WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE binding missing',
44:   });
45: 
46:   const deploymentsColumns = await getTableColumns(env, 'workspace_deployments');
47:   const requiredDeploymentColumns = [
48:     'toolchain_json',
49:     'dependency_cache_key',
50:     'dependency_cache_hit',
51:     'remediations_json',
52:   ];
53:   const missingDeploymentColumns = requiredDeploymentColumns.filter((column) => !deploymentsColumns.includes(column));
54:   checks.push({
55:     code: 'migration_workspace_deployments_0008',
56:     ok: missingDeploymentColumns.length === 0,
57:     details:
58:       missingDeploymentColumns.length === 0
59:         ? 'all phase 6 columns present'
60:         : `missing columns: ${missingDeploymentColumns.join(', ')}`,
61:   });
62: 
63:   const dependencyCacheTableExists = await tableExists(env, 'workspace_dependency_caches');
64:   checks.push({
65:     code: 'migration_workspace_dependency_caches_0008',
66:     ok: dependencyCacheTableExists,
67:     details: dependencyCacheTableExists ? 'table exists' : 'workspace_dependency_caches table is missing',
68:   });
69: 
70:   const flags = await loadRuntimeFlags(env);
71:   checks.push({
72:     code: 'runtime_flag_workspace_deploy_enabled',
73:     ok: flags.workspaceDeployEnabled,
74:     details: flags.workspaceDeployEnabled ? 'enabled' : 'disabled',
75:   });
76: 
77:   const provider = getWorkspaceDeployProviderName(undefined, env);
78:   checks.push({ code: 'workspace_deploy_provider', ok: true, details: provider });
79:   try {
80:     const providerChecks = await createWorkspaceDeployProvider(provider, env).precheck();
81:     checks.push(...providerChecks);
82:   } catch (error) {
83:     const providerError = normalizeProviderError(error);
84:     checks.push({ code: providerError.code, ok: false, details: providerError.message });
85:   }
86: 
87:   return jsonResponse({
88:     ok: checks.every((check) => check.ok),
89:     checks,
90:   });
91: }

state.title: packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts

parts.text:
Found 1 blocking issue in `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts`.

- `provider_scope_missing` is now used for multiple non-scope env failures (`WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PREVIEW_DOMAIN`/`WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME` missing and `WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_REAL_PROVIDER_ENABLED` disabled) at `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts:177`, `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts:182`, and `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts:190`.
- This collides with actual Cloudflare 403 scope errors from `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deploy-provider.ts:220`, so downstream handling cannot distinguish “token lacks scopes” vs “local env misconfigured/feature disabled.”
- Result: callers surface incorrect remediation (grant token scopes) for env/config issues, which is a misclassification bug and blocks reliable operator guidance/automation.

I did not find a blocking issue with the new `success=false` response handling; that path now correctly throws on 2xx with `success: false`.
